chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. D -507 of 2026 __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) Hearing of case 1. For orders on MIT-II report dated 23.02.2026 2. For orders on office objection 3. For hearing of main case 24.02.2026 Mr. Muhammad Zareef Lakho, Advocate along with the petitioners. Mr. Hakim Ali Shaikh, Addl. A.G. Sindh. Mr. Zahoor Ahmed Shah, APG along with Raza Mian, DSP Legal-II, CPO, SIP Naseem Khan, PS Shah Latif Town and SIP Hadi Bux Rind, PS Shah Latif Town. ------------------------------------ 1. Learned counsel for the petitioners submits that petitioner No.1 being sui juris, without any coercion or force had contracted marriage with the petitioner No.2 and now she is performing her marital obligations and living with petitioner No.2 with her consent. Learned counsel further submits that relatives of the petitioner i.e. respondent Nos. 7 to 14 more particularly respondent No.6, who is the mother of petitioner No.1, being antagonized upon marriage of the petitioners, lodged FIR No. 06/2026, under Sections 365-B, 506/2 PPC at Police Station Khorwah, hence prayed for quashment of the above said FIR and also to provide necessary protection of life, honour and property. 2. The I.O. present before the Court has recorded statement of petitioner No.1. In her statement, petitioner No.1 has categorically denied having been taken away by anyone, including petitioner No.2. She has further stated that the allegations levelled by respondent No.6 to 14 are nothing but a bundle of lies, therefore, she seeks quashment of FIR in question. 3.

In her statement, petitioner No.1 has categorically denied having been taken away by anyone, including petitioner No.2. She has further stated that the allegations levelled by respondent No.6 to 14 are nothing but a bundle of lies, therefore, she seeks quashment of FIR in question. 3. The I.O. submits that after thorough investigation the FIR in question was disposed of under “C” Class, however, he will submit the report before the Court having jurisdiction on the basis of statement of petitioner No.1 recorded by him today in Court. 4. Learned counsel for the petitioners being satisfied with the above statement sought disposal of instant petition with directions to the official respondents to provide necessary protection to the petitioners in the manner that no one shall cause them harassment. 5. In view of whatever discussed above, instant petition stands disposed of with directions to official respondents more particularly respondent Nos. 6 to 14 to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and provide necessary legal protection of life, liberty, honour and property to petitioners in the manner that no one shall cause them harassment. I.O present in Court is directed to submit report in view of the statement of petitioner No.1 before the concerned Magistrate. Office is directed to fax the copy of this order to respondents for compliance. Learned MIT-II to ensure compliance. JUDGE HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES JUDGE Azeem
